commit.message:
feat: add CLI interactive mode, per-datatype latency, per-tenant rate limiting, deployment docs

Enhancements:

1. CLI Interactive Mode:
   - Add REPL with readline support, tab completion, command history
   - Commands: status, pause, resume, force-sync, reset-circuit-breaker, etc.
   - History persisted to ~/.inandout_history
   - Usage: inandout interactive --database-url $DATABASE_URL

2. Per-Datatype Latency Metrics:
   - Add sync_duration_seconds histogram with datatype labels
   - Add http_request_duration_seconds histogram for per-endpoint tracking
   - Tracks operation duration for observability and performance tuning

3. Per-Tenant Rate Limiting:
   - Add rate_limit field to AccountConfig for per-tenant overrides
   - Extended get_rate_limiter() to support account_id parameter
   - Separate token buckets per (connector, account_id) tuple
   - Prevents one tenant from starving others in multi-tenant deployments

4. Deployment Documentation:
   - Add comprehensive DEPLOYMENT.md covering Docker and Kubernetes
   - Includes HA setup, scaling considerations, security best practices
   - Covers monitoring, alerting, backup/recovery procedures

5. Troubleshooting Documentation:
   - Add TROUBLESHOOTING.md runbook for operators
   - Covers common issues: sync lag, webhooks, conflicts, performance
   - Includes diagnostic commands and resolution steps

All 2,712 tests passing. Ready for production deployment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  * Support dual-secret verification during rotation period
  * Try primary secret first, fall back to rotation secret
  * Log when rotation secret successfully verifies

feat: implement circuit breaker backoff, reconciliation pass, and streaming responses

**Circuit Breaker Exponential Backoff:**
- Added exponential backoff when circuit breaker reopens from half_open state
- Configurable backoff_multiplier (default 2.0) and max_recovery_timeout (1 hour)
- Each failed recovery attempt doubles the timeout up to the cap
- Successful recovery resets backoff to base timeout
- Per GOAL.md circuit breaker lifecycle requirements

**Reconciliation Pass (T1 #38):**
- Implemented post-pagination reconciliation to detect drift during sync
- Samples up to 100 records and re-queries them individually
- Compares hashes to detect changes that occurred during fetch
- Warns if >10% of sample changed (rapid data churn)
- Enabled via reconciliation_pass: true config flag
- Requires detail_path for individual record fetching

**Streaming Response Support:**
- Added streaming: true flag to ListConfig
- Supports three formats:
  * NDJSON: newline-delimited JSON (most efficient)
  * SSE: Server-Sent Events with data: lines
  * json_array: streaming JSON arrays with batching
- Uses httpx streaming API for memory-efficient processing
- Ideal for real-time APIs, CDC streams, and large result sets

All three features include comprehensive unit tests (13 new tests).
Total test count: 2,469 passing tests (2,456 + 13 new).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

to delete all records for a specific external_id across:

Purge is immediate and takes precedence over retention windows.
Supports compliance with GDPR right-to-erasure requirements.

tracking operator who issued each command. All commands logged with
status (pending → acknowledged → completed/failed).

commit.message:
feat: add delta-only source protection, datatype kind validation, and read-only support (T1 #14, #22, #23)

**Delta-Only Source Protection (T1 #14):**
Added delta_only flag to IngestionConfig. When enabled, prevents watermark
resets via force_full_sync command to protect against data loss. Delta-only
sources (change streams with no full snapshots) would irretrievably lose
data if the stream position is reset.

**Datatype Kind Validation (T1 #22):**
Enhanced validation for relationship datatypes. The kind field now supports
'entity' and 'relationship' values. Relationship datatypes represent
many-to-many associations (memberships, links) as first-class entities with
their own ingestion and writeback pipelines.

**Read-Only Datatype Support (T1 #23):**
Datatypes can now be ingestion-only (no writeback config), writeback-only
(no ingestion), or bidirectional (both). Validation ensures at least one
direction is configured. This enables proper handling of read-only API
endpoints that have no writable counterpart.

All features include comprehensive unit tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: in-and-out
repository.description: Declarative, bidirectional HTTP API synchronisation for Master Data Management.

commit.message:
feat: implement pagination drift protection with server snapshots (T1 #38)

Added snapshot extraction from API responses to mitigate pagination drift.
When snapshot_response_path is configured, the first page response is
inspected to extract a snapshot ID (e.g., timestamp, token, cursor freeze).
This ID is then injected into all subsequent page requests via the
snapshot_param query parameter, ensuring consistent result sets across
paginated fetches even when data is added/deleted concurrently.

Supported across all pagination strategies: cursor, offset, page_number,
link_header, and keyset.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: in-and-out
repository.description: Declarative, bidirectional HTTP API synchronisation for Master Data Management.

feat: implement field coupling for conflict resolution (T2 #3)

When merge_fields conflict resolution is used, coupled_fields config
allows grouping logically-related fields together. When any field in
a group conflicts, all fields in that group are treated as conflicted.

Example use case: [['email', 'email_verified']] ensures email and
verification status stay consistent - if server changes email, the
verification flag is also taken from server even if it didn't change.

Supports multiple independent coupling groups per datatype.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: in-and-out
repository.description: Declarative, bidirectional HTTP API synchronisation for Master Data Management.

commit.message:
feat: implement API deprecation warnings at daemon startup

Implements API deprecation warnings (T1 #39 from GOAL.md).

Features:
- Checks api_version_deprecation_date at daemon startup
- Warns when deprecation date is within api_version_warning_days (default 60 days)
- Errors when deprecation date has passed
- Supports legacy api_deprecation_deadline field
- Per-connector warnings logged with structured logging
- Configurable warning window via api_version_warning_days

Benefits:
- Proactive warning before API versions are deprecated
- Prevents surprise breaking changes
- Clear visibility into upcoming API migrations
- Helps operators plan upgrades

Changes:
- src/inandout/ingestion/daemon.py: Added _check_api_deprecations() function,
  called during connector loading phase
- src/inandout/config/connector.py: Config fields already existed

commit.message:
feat: implement buffer_and_reorder strategy for out-of-order events

Implements buffer_and_reorder strategy for out-of-order event handling (T1 #35 from GOAL.md).

Features:
- EventBuffer class buffers events that arrive out of sequence
- Releases events in order once gaps are filled or timeout expires
- Per-entity buffering isolated by (connector, datatype, external_id)
- Configurable timeout (default 30s) and buffer size (default 100 events)
- Automatic eviction when buffer is full
- Integrated into webhook handler for full-payload webhooks

Benefits:
- Properly handles event streams with reordering
- Prevents data corruption from out-of-order updates
- Graceful timeout handling for permanently missing events
- Memory-safe with buffer size limits

Changes:
- src/inandout/config/ingestion.py: Added buffer_and_reorder to OutOfOrderStrategy,
  added buffer_timeout_secs and buffer_size config fields
- src/inandout/ingestion/event_buffer.py: New module with EventBuffer class
- src/inandout/ingestion/webhooks.py: Integrated buffer_and_reorder strategy
  into webhook handler
- 10 unit tests covering buffering, gap filling, timeouts, and edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement adaptive rate limiting

Implements adaptive rate limiting (T1 #18 from GOAL.md).

- TokenBucket now supports dynamic rate reduction in response to Retry-After
  headers from 429 responses
- When API signals rate limit, bucket temporarily reduces refill rate to 50%
  of base rate for the specified duration, then gradually recovers
- Tokens are drained on backoff to force immediate throttling
- HttpTransportAdapter automatically applies backoff when receiving 429 with
  Retry-After header

Benefits:
- Prevents sustained failures when APIs signal rate limits
- Gracefully backs off instead of hammering rate-limited endpoints
- Automatic recovery after backoff period expires

Changes:
- src/inandout/transport/rate_limiter.py: Added apply_backoff() method,
  adaptive rate tracking, and recovery logic
- src/inandout/transport/http.py: Call apply_backoff() on 429 responses
- 7 new unit tests covering backoff application, recovery, and edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(links): /index.html-Links durch saubere Verzeichnis-URLs ersetzen

Alle internen href-Verweise auf */index.html wurden durch das
äquivalente Trailing-Slash-Format ersetzt, damit der 301-Redirect
(index.html → /) nicht unnötig durchlaufen wird:

  ensemble/{{slug}}/index.html → ensemble/{{slug}}/
  karte/index.html             → karte/
  impressum/index.html         → impressum/
  ../index.html                → ../
  ../../index.html             → ../../

Betroffen: index.html, karte.html, ensemble.html, impressum.html
und build-pipeline.mjs (Karten-URLs, Bildquellen-Liste).
Reine Dateisystempfade im Build-Skript (path.join, outPath) bleiben
unverändert.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(htaccess): 301 /index.html → / für kanonische URL

Apache mod_dir kann / extern auf /index.html umleiten, obwohl die
kanonische URL https://musik-in-schaumburg.de/ lautet. Die neue Regel
leitet explizite Anfragen an /index.html per 301 zurück auf /;
interne DirectoryIndex-Verarbeitung durch Apache bleibt davon unberührt.

Reihenfolge: nach HTTPS/www-Redirects, vor pre-compressed serving.

commit.message:
feat: implement field selection (properties request)

Implements declarative field/property selection (A4 from GOAL.md T1 #21).

- Added properties parameter interpolation into HTTP requests
- Supports three encoding formats:
  * comma: ?properties=field1,field2,field3 (default)
  * array: ?properties=field1&properties=field2 (multiple params)
  * json_array: ?properties=["field1","field2"] (JSON-encoded)

- Works with all pagination strategies (cursor, offset, page_number,
  link_header, keyset)
- Reduces bandwidth and improves performance for large payloads
- 5 new unit tests covering all formats and pagination strategies

Fields affected:
- src/inandout/config/ingestion.py: properties, properties_param,
  properties_format already defined (A4 marker)
- src/inandout/transport/http.py: fetch_pages() now interpolates
  properties into query params based on format

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: in-and-out
repository.description: Declarative, bidirectional HTTP API synchronisation for Master Data Management.

commit.message:
feat: implement CustomAuth, OAuth2 refresh token, and rotate-credential command

- CustomAuthProvider: multi-step HTTP auth flow with token caching, 401
  retry, credential placeholder substitution, and configurable injection
  (header or query parameter). Implements T1 #24 from GOAL.md.

- OAuth2RefreshTokenAuth: authorization_code grant with refresh_token
  exchange, rotated refresh token persistence, proactive expiry refresh,
  and refresh_url fallback. Implements T1 #11 from GOAL.md.

- rotate-credential control command: invalidates all cached auth tokens
  for a credential_ref across OAuth2, refresh token, and custom auth
  caches. Added to ControlDispatcher and CLI _VALID_COMMANDS.

- invalidate_credential_cache() utility for cross-provider cache clearing.

- 33 new unit tests covering all three features.

commit.message:
Add Thermal Barrier Coating (TBC) analysis system

Materials module:
- TBCCoating dataclass with conductivity, thickness, emissivity, cost
- 7 coating database: YSZ standard/EB-PVD, Gadolinium Zirconate,
  Diatomite-Silica, Prismatic Chitin, Nacre-Layered, Ceramic Aerogel
- 1-D steady-state heat transfer model (gas -> TBC -> substrate)
- Per-component analysis with margin improvement calculation
- Full comparison across all coatings
- Bio-inspired coatings (0.015-0.06 W/mK) vs conventional (1.5-2.0 W/mK)

Results (Diatomite-Silica, +5.7g):
  Combustor: 890K -> 443K (margin +83K -> +530K)
  Turbine:   858K -> 451K (margin +115K -> +522K)
  Nozzle:    752K -> 397K (margin +501K -> +856K)

API: /api/tbc_analysis (single coating or compare all)
Dashboard: TBC panel with coating selector, bar chart, comparison mode
Inspired by Reddit user bio-material suggestion (silver ant / diatomite)

docs(copilot): Dateibenennungsschema für Bilder konkretisieren

Statt generischer Namen (photo.jpg, logo.png) die tatsächlich verwendete
Konvention dokumentieren:
- Hauptbild: <slug>.jpg (bzw. .png / .webp passend zum Dateiformat)
- Logo:      logo-<slug>.png (bzw. .jpg)
Außerdem: Hinweis ergänzt, dass die Dateiendung dem tatsächlichen
Format entsprechen muss (ggf. umbenennen).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ensemble): Posaunenchor Bad Nenndorf (gegr. 1913 als Posaunenchor Kreuzriehe)

Fixes #116

Gegründet am 21. Januar 1913 unter dem Namen »Posaunenchor Kreuzriehe« in
Kreuzriehe; heute Posaunenchor der ev. St.-Godehardi-Gemeinde Bad Nenndorf.

- Typ: posaunenchor
- Gründungsjahr: 1913 (historischer Name Kreuzriehe in Beschreibung erwähnt)
- Leitung: Alessa Holzapfel
- Probenort: Haus der Begegnung, Hauptstraße 36, 31542 Bad Nenndorf
- Proben: dienstags 19:00 Uhr
- Website: kirche-bad-nenndorf.de
- Kontakt: E-Mail + Telefon
- Geo: 52.3374, 9.3814
- Tag: Kirche
- Foto: (c) Posaunenchor Bad Nenndorf / ev. Kirche St. Godehardi
- Logo: (c) ev. Kirche St. Godehardi (aus Gemeindeinfo-PDF)
- EXIF aus beiden Bilddateien entfernt

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add extern "C" guards to all SDK header files for C++ compatibility

Wrap all public SDK headers (src/sdk/include/*.h) and libc shim headers
(src/sdk/libc/*.h) with #ifdef __cplusplus / extern "C" blocks so that
C++ translation units can include them without name-mangling issues.

For include headers: guards wrap all content between the include guard
#define and the final #endif.

For libc headers: guards wrap only the #else (non-OF_PC) block,
leaving #include_next directives outside the extern "C" scope.

Exception files not modified (macros/typedefs only):
stdint.h, limits.h, inttypes.h, fcntl.h, assert.h, malloc.h, SDL2/SDL.h

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Dependabot security alerts #45, #47, #48: upgrade npm and Python build dependencies (#1065)

* Initial plan

* Upgrade mocha and add overrides to fix npm security vulnerabilities

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/d7dbd434-2d27-4a08-914d-d8410e3a63e4

* Address Dependabot alerts: upgrade setuptools and actions/checkout

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/fe063606-4785-4a11-ba16-6dcbdaaa7c80

* Drop Python 3.8 support: incompatible with setuptools>=78.1.1

setuptools 78.x requires Python >= 3.9, so building for Python 3.8
fails after the setuptools minimum was raised to 78.1.1. Remove 3.8
from the CI test matrix and all release scripts (Linux, macOS, Windows),
and update the twine upload environments from py3.8 to py3.9.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Carbo Kuo <BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
